Title: Plasmopara viticola Effector PvRXLR10 Targets a Host Phospholipase VvipPLA-IIδ2 to Suppress Plant Immunity in Grapevine

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for transient overexpression in N. benthamiana and grapevine; pBWA(V)HS-RNAi hairpin construct for transient silencing
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Thompson Seedless (functional assays); Cabernet Sauvignon (cDNA library source)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: VvipPLA-IIδ2 is a positive immunity-associated lipid metabolism gene and a promising target for engineering/downy mildew resistance breeding. Practical use could include overexpression, promoter editing to enhance pathogen-inducible expression, or allele mining/selection for stronger activity while avoiding constitutive defense costs. Because it likely acts through jasmonic-acid-related lipid signaling, stacking with JA-pathway defense genes may improve resistance; however, caution is needed because excessive JA activation can cause growth-defense tradeoffs and this study did not measure agronomic performance or metabolite flux directly.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes grapevine resistance to Plasmopara viticola; overexpression decreases sporulation and elevates expression of jasmonic acid biosynthesis, signaling, and defense genes, whereas RNAi increases susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and RNAi silencing in grapevine; pathogen infection assays with sporangia counting; yeast two-hybrid, GST pull-down, BiFC and co-IP for interaction with PvRXLR10; phospholipase A2 activity assay; subcellular localization; RT-qPCR of JA-related genes.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plasmopara viticola infection/downy mildew challenge; transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression; greenhouse growth at 25°C under 16 h light/8 h dark.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during early pathogen challenge; previously reported to increase at 12 h post infection after pathogen challenge; in this study its transcript was suppressed by PvRXLR10 during P. viticola infection.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species because the gene is native to grapevine; induced early upon pathogen infection.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library screening from P. viticola-infected Vitis vinifera Cabernet Sauvignon leaves; prior transcriptomic/genomic resources for P. viticola are cited, but no accession number reported for this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to lipid-derived JA-associated immunity, the paper did not directly quantify JA, JA-Ile, linolenic acid, 12-OPDA, or phospholipid metabolites; therefore nutrient-metabolism linkage is inferred from PLA2 activity and JA-marker transcription. Localizes partly to ER. Classified here as Common_Genes because the paper emphasizes immune/lipid signaling contribution rather than full enzymatic pathway mapping to a nutrient product.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly assessed. Potential risk of constitutive JA-pathway activation causing growth-defense tradeoffs, off-target defense activation, or altered membrane lipid homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Patatin-like phospholipase A / patatin_and_cPLA2 subfamily; Pat17_isozyme-like domain; GTSTG esterase box; phospholipase A2 activity
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated; perinuclear ER and ER network; some bright cytoplasmic spots reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvipPLA-IIδ2 encodes a patatin-like phospholipase with measurable cPLA2 activity that hydrolyzes fatty acids from the sn-2 position of glycerophospholipids. 2. Overexpression enhances, while RNAi silencing reduces, grapevine resistance to P. viticola. 3. VvipPLA-IIδ2 overexpression upregulates jasmonic acid biosynthesis, signaling, and defense-marker genes, supporting a causal role in JA-associated lipid metabolism and immunity. 4. The pathogen effector PvRXLR10 targets and suppresses VvipPLA-IIδ2, likely weakening this lipid-derived defense pathway.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling against pathogen infection, especially contributing to resistance to downy mildew and broader immune responses.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AOS is a useful marker and potential engineering target for strengthening JA-mediated disease resistance. It may be stacked with upstream lipid-release genes such as VvipPLA-IIδ2 or downstream JA signaling components, but constitutive activation may impose defense-growth tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxides derived from α-linolenic acid into unstable allene oxide intermediates in the octadecanoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its transcript is upregulated in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves, consistent with enhanced JA biosynthetic capacity and defense activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct intermediate en route to JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VvipPLA-IIδ2 overexpression; comparison at 5 days post infiltration; pathogen-defense context from study framework.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species context only; detailed native pattern not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a JA pathway gene measured transcriptionally; no direct functional perturbation of AOS itself was performed in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not assessed here; elevated JA biosynthesis may affect growth-defense balance.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Fatty acid hydroperoxide from α-linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme, but rate-limiting status not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Not reported in this paper (classically chloroplast-associated in JA pathway)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS transcript increases in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves. 2. This supports activation of the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway downstream of phospholipid hydrolysis. 3. The paper uses AOS as evidence linking VvipPLA-IIδ2-mediated lipid metabolism to defense-associated JA production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and activation of pathogen-responsive immunity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AOC may serve as a downstream engineering or marker target to enhance JA formation in disease-resistance programs, especially in stacks with phospholipases and JA signaling regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes the unstable allene oxide intermediate to form 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid precursor intermediates in JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOC transcript is significantly increased in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves, indicating stimulation of JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct intermediate en route to JA production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VvipPLA-IIδ2 overexpression at 5 days post infiltration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: General AOC plus AOC1 and AOC2 were all monitored; gene nomenclature may reflect multiple family members in grapevine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC expression rises in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves. 2. This supports enhanced flux through the JA biosynthetic pathway. 3. The result contributes transcriptional evidence that VvipPLA-IIδ2 positively influences oxylipin metabolism linked to immunity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AOC1 is a candidate marker/target for boosting JA-mediated defense, though direct engineering value was not tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cyclization of allene oxide to produce JA-pathway cyclopentenone intermediate(s) leading to 12-OPDA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is elevated in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing leaves, consistent with enhanced JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor in the JA branch.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as a specific JA-biosynthetic family member; no direct mutant or enzymatic test in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC1 transcript is induced by VvipPLA-IIδ2 overexpression. 2. This supports increased JA precursor formation. 3. It serves as part of the molecular evidence for VvipPLA-IIδ2-mediated activation of lipid-derived defense metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AOC2 is another potential marker or companion engineering target for activating JA-dependent immunity, but direct utility requires testing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes allene oxide to produce oxylipin intermediates leading toward 12-OPDA in the jasmonate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is increased in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as one of several JA pathway markers responsive to VvipPLA-IIδ2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC2 transcript rises when VvipPLA-IIδ2 is overexpressed. 2. This is consistent with stimulated jasmonate precursor biosynthesis. 3. The gene was used as transcriptional evidence for activation of JA metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OPR3 is a classic JA-pathway engineering node and useful marker for resistance breeding programs focused on jasmonate competence. It may be stacked with upstream lipid-release steps.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid (12-OPDA) in the JA biosynthetic pathway toward jasmonic acid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript is significantly upregulated in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing leaves, indicating enhanced JA biosynthetic flux.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a late precursor directly leading to JA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly cites 12-OPDA as precursor of JA and uses OPR3 induction as evidence of elevated JA pathway activity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced cyclopentenone intermediate leading to JA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late JA-pathway enzyme; not established as rate-limiting here.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 transcript accumulation increases upon VvipPLA-IIδ2 overexpression. 2. This supports enhanced conversion of JA precursors in the octadecanoid pathway. 3. Together with other pathway markers, it links phospholipase activity to jasmonate-associated defense metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: JAR1 is a useful target for increasing bioactive JA-Ile formation and defense responsiveness. It may be an attractive stacking partner with VvipPLA-IIδ2, though constitutive activation may penalize growth.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates jasmonic acid to isoleucine to form the bioactive hormone jasmonoyl-isoleucine (JA-Ile).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JAR1 transcript is significantly induced in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves, implying elevated JA activation to JA-Ile.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of the active hormone derivative JA-Ile from JA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The authors infer that both JA and JA-Ile synthesis are increased in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing plants, but neither metabolite was directly measured.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not assessed; elevated JA-Ile signaling could impose defense-growth costs.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase family
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important activation step; control-point status not established here.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAR1 transcript is elevated in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing leaves. 2. The result suggests increased capacity to generate bioactive JA-Ile. 3. This supports the model that VvipPLA-IIδ2 enhances jasmonate-mediated defense signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive jasmonate signal for defense gene activation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not directly manipulated in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: JAZ1 is a useful readout and possible regulatory engineering target for tuning JA signaling, but because JAZ proteins are central repressors in jasmonate homeostasis, manipulation requires caution to avoid complex pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JAZ1 transcript is upregulated in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing leaves, indicating altered/activated JA signaling status during enhanced defense response.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-responsive JA signaling regulator; used here as evidence that the jasmonate signaling network is activated downstream of VvipPLA-IIδ2.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely part of canonical JA feedback regulation, but this was not directly examined in the paper.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: JAZ1 was not functionally perturbed in this study; it is included because it is a key regulatory marker gene directly tied to the target metabolite class (jasmonates).
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating JAZ repressors can have broad pleiotropic effects on growth and stress responses; not evaluated here.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JA-responsive transcriptional programs such as defense genes; specific direct targets not tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Canonical JAZ corepressor complexes are known generally, but no complex was experimentally tested in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression module within JA signaling; transcript accumulation used here as marker of pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with JA pathway regulation; exact downstream targets not tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ1 expression increases in VvipPLA-IIδ2-overexpressing grapevine leaves. 2. This indicates altered jasmonate signaling downstream of phospholipase-mediated lipid metabolism. 3. The result supports the model that VvipPLA-IIδ2 positively influences JA-dependent defense regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and immune response modulation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: VvipPLA-IIδ2 overexpression; likely increased JA/JA-Ile, though not directly quantified.
